commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=219cbbed046d89c221e7c47f459ec01291f57ed2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a directory-symlink capability probe and changes four refresh watcher symlink tests from unconditional non-Windows guards to capability-based skips.

PR surface: Tests +27. Total +27 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection is enough to reproduce the review defect: the PR enables tests using a junction probe while the test setup still asks Node for `"dir"` symlinks. I did not run a Windows host, so this is source-reproducible rather than locally reproduced.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed to repair the patch and provide real behavior proof; ClawSweeper repair should not be requested while the external proof gate is missing.

target.title: test: make refresh symlink tests compatible with Windows
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where several workspace-load symlink boundary tests were unconditionally skipped on Windows environments, even if the environment supports creating file and directory symlinks (like with Developer Mode enabled).

## Why This Change Was Made

Replaces the hardcoded `process.platform !== "win32"` skips with dynamic capability checks (`canCreateDirectorySymlinks` and `canCreateFileSymlinks`). If file and directory symlinks are supported by the environment, the tests execute. Otherwise, they skip gracefully while keeping coverage active on capable hosts.

## User Impact

No user-visible product impact. This improves test portability and preserves regression coverage on capable Windows environments.

## Evidence

- Tests run on Windows when directory junctions are supported.
- `vitest run src/skills/loading/workspace-load.test.ts` passes.
- Code conforms to existing symlink capability patterns.
